one_time_description: Comprehensive Settlement Agreement (CSA) trust fund receivable of $20,400,000 related to 2011 flooding damages, settled with Province of Manitoba and Government of Canada
economic_notes: Band operates multiple enterprises including Arena and Lottery, Grocery Store and Gas Bar, Motel and Entertainment Centre, Powder Horn Creek Restaurant and Gas. COVID-19 pandemic impacts noted. 25 VLT terminals secured against Manitoba Liquor and Lotteries loan.
notes: Large one-time item: $20.4M CSA trust fund receivable recognized related to 2011 flooding settlement with Province of Manitoba and Canada, finalized June 2021 after year-end. This drove the large annual surplus of $36.2M. Federal capital transfers also unusually high at $32.55M vs $6.1M prior year, largely related to housing capital projects. Net financial position swung from net debt of $18.26M to net financial assets of $13.12M. 9 consolidated subsidiaries including Housing Trust. Demand loan of $4.48M converted to 5-year term loan in 2021. No schedule of remuneration included in provided statements. Auditor identified as 'Chartered Professional Accountants Inc.' based in Winnipeg, Manitoba — firm name not fully specified. Prior year government transfers restated.
